briefdescription.para: 2D array containing precomputed filter coefficients for a 6-tap Lanczos2 horizontal scaler.

detaileddescription.para: This constant array holds the coefficients for each phase of the horizontal scaling operation, using a 6-tap Lanczos2 filter. The array is indexed first by the phase (from 0 to XV_HSCALER_MAX_H_PHASES-1), and then by the tap index (from 0 to XV_HSCALER_TAPS_6-1).
These coefficients are used by the horizontal scaler hardware or software to perform high-quality image resampling with minimal artifacts.

briefdescription.para: 2D array containing precomputed filter coefficients for a 6-tap Lanczos2 horizontal scaler.

detaileddescription.para: This constant array holds the coefficients for each phase of the horizontal scaling operation, using a 6-tap Lanczos2 filter. The array is indexed first by the phase (from 0 to XV_HSCALER_MAX_H_PHASES-1), and then by the tap index (from 0 to XV_HSCALER_TAPS_6-1).
These coefficients are used by the horizontal scaler hardware or software to perform high-quality image resampling with minimal artifacts.

briefdescription.para: This function determines the internal coefficient table to be used based on scaling ratio and loads the filter coefficients in the scaler coefficient storage.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is the input stream width
is the output stream width

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_Hscaler_l2 *HscPtr, u32 WidthIn, u32 WidthOut, u32 PixelRate)
memberdef.name: CalculatePhases

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the phases for 1 line.

detaileddescription.para: Same phase info is used for full frame

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is the input frame width
is the scaled frame width
is the number of pixels per clock being processed

briefdescription.para: This function programs the filter coefficients and phase data into core registers.

detaileddescription.para:
HscPtr
is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
None This version of driver does not make use of computed coefficients. User must load the coefficients, using the provided API, before scaler can be used

briefdescription.para: This function programs the phase data into core registers.

detaileddescription.para:
HscPtr
is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
None This version of driver does not make use of computed coefficients. User must load the coefficients, using the provided API, before scaler can be used

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the core instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is instance id of the core

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if device is found and initialized XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if device is not found

briefdescription.para: This function starts the horizontal scaler core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the horizontal scaler core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_Hscaler_l2 *InstancePtr, u16 num_phases, u16 num_taps, const short *Coeff)
memberdef.name: XV_HScalerLoadExtCoeff

briefdescription.para: This function loads user defined filter coefficients in the scaler coefficient storage.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is the number of phases in coefficient table
is the number of taps in coefficient table
is a pointer to user defined filter coefficients table

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_Hscaler_l2 *InstancePtr, u32 HeightIn, u32 WidthIn, u32 WidthOut, u32 ColorFormatIn, u32 ColorFormatOut)
memberdef.name: XV_HScalerSetup

briefdescription.para: This function configures the scaler core registers with the specified configuration parameters of the axi stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is the input stream height
is the input stream width
is the output stream width
is the input stream color format
is the output stream color format

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the requested setup parameters are valid XST_FAILURE otherwise

briefdescription.para: This function prints H Scaler status on the console.

parameterdescription.para: is the instance pointer to the core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the given input and output color formats are valid configuration parameters for this instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance to be worked on.
is the input stream color format.
is the output stream color format.

simplesect.para: TRUE if the requested setup parameters are valid, FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This file provides the default fixed coefficient sets for supported taps.

detaileddescription.para: This header file contains layer 2 API's of the horizontal scaler sub-core driver.The functions contained herein provides a high level implementation of features provided by the IP, abstracting away the register level details from the user.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 rco 07/31/15 Initial Release 3.1 rco 02/09/17 Fix c++ compilation warnings 3.3 vsa 04/07/20 Improve quality with better coefficients

detaileddescription.para:
This H-Scaler IP supports following features
3 Channel Scaler with RGB, YUV444 and optional YUV422, and YUV420 support
Scale horizontally to 4K line at 60Hz
up to 16bits color depth

This driver makes use of the video enumerations and data types defined in the Xilinx Video Common Driver (video_common_vX.x) and as such the common driver must be included as dependency to compile this driver
The device driver enables higher layer software (e.g., an application) to communicate to the hscaler core.

Before using the layer-2 API's user must initialize the core by calling Layer-1 API XV_hscaler_Initialize(). This function will look for a configuration structure for the device and initialize it to defined HW settings. After initialization Layer-2 API's can be used to configure the core. It is recommended user always make use of Layer-2 API to interact with this core. Advanced users always have the capability to directly interact with the IP core using Layer-1 API's that perform low level register peek/poke.
This driver does not have any interrupts
This driver supports Virtual Memory. The RTOS is responsible for calculating the correct device base address in Virtual Memory space.
This driver is not thread safe. Any needs for threads or thread mutual exclusion must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 rco 07/21/15 Initial Release 2.00 rco 11/05/15 Integrate layer-1 with layer-2 dmc 12/17/15 Add macro to query the Is422Enabled flag that was added to the XV_hscaler_Config structure 3.0 mpe 04/28/16 Added optional color format conversion handling
